name: Mendoza
summary: Mendoza is considered one of Argentina's safer cities, known for its relaxed atmosphere and welcoming locals. While petty crime like pickpocketing occurs, particularly in tourist areas and markets, violent crime against tourists is relatively rare.
quickVerdict: Mendoza offers a safe travel experience for most visitors, with lower crime rates compared to Buenos Aires and other major South American cities. The wine capital maintains a laid-back atmosphere with friendly locals, though travelers should remain alert for opportunistic crimes and exercise standard urban precautions. The city's compact downtown, strong police presence, and tourism infrastructure make it particularly suitable for first-time visitors to South America.

neighborhoods.name: Central Mendoza (Microcentro)
neighborhoods.description: Downtown commercial core around Plaza Independencia with good tourist services, restaurants, and visible police presence, though petty theft can occur in crowded areas.

neighborhoods.name: Chacras de Coria
neighborhoods.description: Upscale suburban area with excellent restaurants, local markets, and proximity to wineries. One of the safest areas near Mendoza with limited nightlife but well-lit streets.

neighborhoods.name: Lujan de Cuyo
neighborhoods.description: Wine country area popular with tourists, featuring numerous bodegas and upscale accommodations. Generally very safe during the day with good lighting on main roads.

neighborhoods.name: Godoy Cruz
neighborhoods.description: Neighboring municipality with mixed residential and commercial areas. Safe in central parts but requires caution in peripheral zones, especially after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Guaymallen
neighborhoods.description: Densely populated area with some working-class neighborhoods. Exercise caution, particularly in outer areas at night where petty theft has been reported.

neighborhoods.name: Las Heras (peripheral areas)
neighborhoods.description: Some northern areas require extra caution due to higher crime rates. Avoid poorly lit streets after dark and stay in well-populated central zones.

scams.name: Distraction Theft at Tourist Sites
scams.description: Thieves work in pairs to distract tourists at wineries, markets, or busy plazas while an accomplice steals belongings, phones, or wallets.
scams.howToAvoid: Stay alert in crowded areas, keep bags secured and in front of you, and be wary of overly helpful strangers or staged distractions.

scams.name: Bus Station Luggage Scam
scams.description: Criminals target travelers at Mendoza's bus terminal, either through staged falls over luggage or helpful offers to carry bags, resulting in theft during the confusion.
scams.howToAvoid: Keep luggage close at all times, decline unsolicited help, and remain vigilant around the bus station area, especially when boarding or departing.

scams.name: Fake Police Document Check
scams.description: Individuals posing as police officers approach tourists requesting to see documents and money, claiming to check for counterfeit bills or drugs.
scams.howToAvoid: Ask for official identification, request to go to a police station, and never hand over cash or valuables. Real police will have proper uniforms and badges.

scams.name: ATM Skimming and Shoulder Surfing
scams.description: Criminals use card readers or watch PIN entry at ATMs, particularly targeting foreign bank cards for unauthorized withdrawals.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ATMs inside banks or hotels, shield your PIN entry, check for unusual devices on card slots, and monitor your account regularly.

soloFemale.overview: Mendoza is generally considered safe for solo female travelers and ranks well compared to other Argentine cities. Women report positive experiences in the wine regions, city center, and tourist areas. However, standard precautions should be taken, particularly at night and in less populated areas.
tips: Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in central areas like downtown Mendoza or Chacras de Coria
Use reputable transportation like Uber, registered taxis, or organized wine tours rather than walking alone at night
Join wine tasting groups or organized activities to meet other travelers and locals safely

nightSafety.overview: Mendoza's nightlife centers around Aristides Villanueva Avenue (Beer Street) and various wine bars, which are generally safe until midnight. The city has a more relaxed nightlife scene compared to Buenos Aires, with most venues staying busy until around 1 AM. Police presence is visible in central areas.
tips: Stick to well-lit, populated streets and main nightlife areas around the city center
Use ride-sharing apps or licensed taxis for transportation after dark, avoid walking alone
Don't display valuable items and keep drinks in sight to prevent tampering

transport.metro: Mendoza has a Metrotram system covering five main departments with 21 stops along 17km of route. It's generally safe and connects key areas including the city center, with capacity for 180 passengers and bicycle storage.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Cabify operate legally in Mendoza since 2018, making it the first Argentine province to authorize ride-sharing. These apps are considered the safest transport option with GPS tracking, driver ratings, and emergency buttons.
transport.taxis: Licensed taxis are black with yellow doors and 'TAXI' signs. Fares start around 130 pesos with meters, though some drivers may take advantage of tourists. Radio taxis and app-based services are more reliable.
transport.tips: Transportation is generally safe and reliable. Use ride-sharing apps when possible for transparency and security features, especially at night or from the airport.

customs: Greet with a single kiss on the right cheek, regardless of gender - this is standard even when meeting for the first time
Dinner is served very late (9-11 PM) with long sobremesa (after-dinner conversation) being an important social tradition
Personal appearance and hygiene are highly valued - dress well and maintain good grooming to earn respect and appreciation

health.overview: Argentina requires all visitors to have health insurance covering medical emergencies and hospitalization. Mendoza has good healthcare facilities, particularly in private hospitals, with many medical professionals speaking English in tourist areas.
health.water: Tap water in Mendoza is generally safe to drink as Argentina has some of the cleanest water quality in South America, though bottled water is recommended for sensitive stomachs.
health.vaccinations: No specific vaccinations are required for Mendoza. Yellow fever vaccination may be recommended for travelers visiting northern Argentina. Routine vaccinations should be up to date.
health.altitude: Mendoza sits at 760 meters elevation with a dry, sunny climate and over 300 days of sunshine per year. The area experiences hot summers and mild winters with minimal altitude-related health concerns.

emergency.usEmbassy: U.S. Embassy Buenos Aires: +54 11 5777-4533 (24-hour emergency line). The embassy is located in Buenos Aires and provides consular services for all of Argentina including Mendoza.

faq.a: Yes, Mendoza is generally considered one of Argentina's safer cities for tourists. It has lower crime rates than Buenos Aires and most South American capitals, with a laid-back atmosphere and strong tourism infrastructure. Standard urban precautions should still be taken.

Central Mendoza is relatively safe at night, especially around the main nightlife area on Aristides Villanueva Avenue. However, it's recommended to use ride-sharing apps or licensed taxis rather than walking alone, and stick to well-lit, populated areas.

faq.q: Is Mendoza safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Mendoza is considered safe for solo female travelers and ranks well among Argentine destinations. Women report positive experiences, though standard precautions like staying in central areas, using reputable transportation, and joining group activities are recommended.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Mendoza?
faq.a: Avoid peripheral areas of Guaymallen and Las Heras at night, poorly lit side streets after dark, and the areas around the bus terminal when traveling with luggage. The downtown area east of the bus station can also be less safe after dark.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Mendoza?
faq.a: Yes, public transportation including the Metrotram and buses is generally safe. However, Uber and Cabify are recommended as the safest options due to their GPS tracking, driver verification, and emergency features. Licensed radio taxis are also reliable.
